Hi, welcome back to the Living Legend Formula video series. My name is Greg Roulette, and once again, I am joined by Emmy Award-winning director, bestselling author, Mr. Nick Nantin. Nick, welcome back. Good to be here, man. I'm glad to be on our familiar, comfortable set that we film our TV shows on. We're going to have some fun today. We are going to have some fun today. And before we get started in this fourth and most critical video in this series, I want to take people back through the journey that we've brought them through, because in the first video, we really explained to them why, who is a very important question. Let's recap on why it's really important to build that personal brand, that celebrity brand, and understand that you need to be the person that comes up when someone goes, who? Absolutely. We talked about it, and we kind of dramatized it in the first video, but a nameless facials brand is always a commodity. If you didn't know Dan Kennedy, you wouldn't pay him for the things you pay for from a newsletter all the way up to days of consulting. So the fact of the matter is, if you want to be in a marketplace free from competition, then you should be building a personal brand. You could build it all the way up to and through the living legend formula, which Dan really revealed to me, which we're excited to reveal even more of today. But the fact of the matter is that if you're competing based on anything other than personality and legend status, then you're competing on something that can be knocked out from underneath you. Yeah, and we're going to talk a lot today about why you don't actually have to be a legend like Dan Kennedy, or like Hugh Hefner, or Disney. We're going to talk about how any business owner, entrepreneur, or expert can use the principles that we're going to teach. Before we dig into that, in video number two, we introduced two important concepts that we teach here. We were over in the classroom, kind of over on the side here, is that the secret formula for media success and also the business trifecta. Let's give a quick overview of those two. So the main thing there that you've got to understand is the business trifecta is the reason why media and PR don't work for most people, because they don't understand that it has to be tied together with a third thing, marketing, which most people watching this or who follow Dan know about. But marketing is what drives revenue. They drive credibility and awareness. That's why you need the two of them to insert in your marketing. And that really is what the secret formula is, is taking mass media credentials and credibility and inserting them in your direct media, which is really direct marketing. Any type of media you create, send to a list, as Dan would call it, a herd of people who are interested who might actually hire you creating media and then using that mass media credentials in that media you create. CDs, DVDs, sales letters, newsletters, magazines, all that stuff, firing it out, but having this credential on top of it so people will actually pay attention. Exactly. I'm not saying anything wrong with that, but we all know that we usually put in typically more than four hours a week. Tim Ferriss would tell you the same if you talk to him. But this is a strategy that you can use to work the same and make more, work less and probably make more, work more and make a lot more. I mean, it's whatever you want to do with it. And this is a really important concept. My neighbors don't probably even know what I do, right? And so it's not something that has to invade your privacy or it's not about ego. And building a personal brand is the easiest way to have an unfair advantage because literally it is the only thing no one else can copy. If a patent, you publish a patent, they'll change one thing and they have it. So it's literally the one thing that you can have that no one else can have. So what I want you to do is think about, look, you may not have to get on video. You may not have to be the most eloquent person speaking. But you can see how he's been able to transform his life and his business by coming out in front of the business. He did most of it, by the way, and he still does most of it in writing. So it's just using personality so people can connect. If people do not connect with you, they're not going to spend money with you. Another thing, Dan, is affinity. People trust people who are like them. So using personality allows you to throw out what I call personal hooks and talk about, you know, you see Dan talk about horse racing and the fact that he was an alcoholic, the fact that he's been married three times twice to the same woman, right? And these things that he reveals to people. He talks about Disney and just goes on and on and on. Right. These are things when you reveal these things through personality, they're little hooks for people out in the marketplace to connect with, bite onto, and then they start swimming in, right? When you get the hook in, you start reeling them in, right? So personality-driven branding is the basis of this, but it doesn't mean you have to be cheesy, it doesn't mean you have to be corny, you don't have to spend millions of dollars.
